heading: National goals and multi-year funding for Federal alcohol from biomass and other technology programs
label: 12003 National goals and multi-year funding for Federal alcohol from biomass and other technology programs

children.label: A
children.text: In general, the goal of the Alcohol From Biomass Program shall be to advance research and development to a point where alcohol from biomass technology is cost-competitive with conventional hydrocarbon transportation fuels, and to promote the integration of this technology into the transportation fuel sector of the economy.

children.label: I
children.text: reduce the cost of alcohol to 70 cents per gallon;

children.label: II
children.text: improve the overall biomass carbohydrate conversion efficiency to 91 percent;

children.label: III
children.text: reduce the capital cost component of the cost of alcohol to 23 cents per gallon; and

children.label: IV
children.text: reduce the operating and maintenance component of the cost of alcohol to 47 cents per gallon.

children.label: I
children.text: reduce the cost of alcohol to 47 cents per gallon; and

children.label: II
children.text: reduce the capital component of the cost of alcohol to 16 cents per gallon.

children.heading: Other technologies
children.label: 2 Other technologies
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Congress, as part of the first report submitted under , recommendations for specific cost goals and other pertinent goals for 1995 for Department of Energy research, development, and demonstration programs in Biofuels Energy Systems, Biodiesel Energy Systems, Hydrogen Energy Systems, Solar Buildings Energy Systems, Marine Energy Systems, Geothermal Energy Systems, Low-Head Hydro, and Energy Storage Systems.section 12006 of this title

children.heading: Amended goals
children.label: b Amended goals
children.text: Whenever the Secretary determines that any of the goals established under this section is no longer appropriate, the Secretary shall notify Congress, as part of a report submitted under , of the reason for the determination and provide an amended goal that is consistent with the purpose stated in .section 12006 of this titlesection 12001(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: not to exceed $19,000,000 shall be available for the Geothermal Energy Systems Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: not to exceed $4,000,000 shall be available for the Hydrogen Energy Systems Program; and

children.label: A
children.text: not to exceed $20,500,000 shall be available for the Geothermal Energy Systems Program; and

children.label: B
children.text: not to exceed $5,000,000 shall be available for the Hydrogen Energy Systems Program.
